name: Sharon's Famous Passover Brownies & Brownie I
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, beat eggs, sugar, cocoa powder, and salt.
2. Add oil to the mixing bowl and blend well. Stir in the matzo cake meal and chocolate pieces. Stir in nuts if desired.
3. Line an 8x8x2-inch baking pan with foil. Grease lightly.
4. Turn batter into prepared pan and spread evenly.
5. Bake at 325°F for 25 to 30 minutes or until done (top will be shiny and edges dry). Let cool in pan on a wire rack.
6. Meanwhile, prepare glaze if desired. Spread over cooled brownies.
Dark Chocolate Glaze:
1. In a small heavy saucepan or skillet, melt 1 cup semisweet chocolate pieces over low heat.
2. Remove from heat and stir in 1 tsp butter or margarine.
3. In a small bowl, dissolve 1/2 tsp instant coffee crystals in 1 tbsp hot water. Stir in 3 tbsp dairy sour cream and 1/4 tsp maple flavoring.
4. Add sour cream mixture to chocolate, stirring until smooth and shiny. Makes about 3/4 cup.
Note: For a regular brownie, you can substitute 3/4 cup all-purpose flour for the matzo cake meal and add 1 tsp vanilla. Also, you can double the recipe. Use a 13x9x2-inch baking pan and bake batter for about 30 minutes.
Brownie Ice Cream Pie:
1. Prepare brownie batter as directed.
2. Line a 9-inch pie plate with foil and grease lightly.
3. Turn batter into pie plate. Bake at 325°F for 30 minutes. Remove from oven.
4. Using the back of a wooden spoon dipped in cocoa powder, press hot brownies down, then gradually outward and evenly up the sides of the pie plate. Cool.
5. Make Dark Chocolate Glaze while crust is freezing; cool glaze to room temperature.
6. Lift shell from plate; remove foil.
7. Fill with 1/2 gal of slightly softened ice cream or frozen yogurt, adding a middle layer of the glaze and more chopped nuts. (If you'd like a smaller pie, use 1 qt of ice cream in the crust, then top with glaze and chopped nuts.)
Sharon Winstein and her friend Louise Levitt created these flour-free brownies for their first temple cooking class. For a twist, make the Brownie Ice Cream Pie.
Source: Midwest Living Magazine, April 1991
